title: 2I Questions
wikitext:
[]: 2I Questions             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Mar 18, 2010 10:08 pm From what I understand of that poorly-explained disaster, the Katsuragi Expedition woke Adam up when they inserted some kind of DNA into him. Adam started up the life-creation process like he was supposed to do, but when that happened, they tried to stop it with the Lance. Somehow or another, that failed and Adam self-destructed. Now, this raises the obvious question of whether or not some or all of the Angels were born on that day, or previously. However, that's not the only thing I was going to ask. When we see the Expedition's bodies lying around, most of them are obviously dead. However, many of them all look like they've been shredded with something sharp, not necessarily crushed. Then we see Dr. Katsuragi, and he looks like he's been through a blender. Misato almost looks like something tried to disembowel her before her father saved her. Did Adam do that before getting even bigger and exploding, or was there something else running around cutting people apart before the big bang?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Mar 18, 2010 10:32 pm The Anti-AT Field was responsible, I believe. Or at least, that's what I got from Evacommentary and the Wiki.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Thu Mar 18, 2010 10:36 pm There was a lot of stuff that exploded during the catastrophe, so I'd imagine it's quite possible that people got shredded, torn open, horribly burned, and crushed by all sorts of debris and hazardous materials.

[]: Re: 2I Questions             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Mar 18, 2010 10:42 pm I doubt the injuries of the expedition team members are from anything other than general disaster related incidents, or possible the effects of Adam's AATF or changing or the atmospheric components. CyberXIII wrote: Now, this raises the obvious question of whether or not some or all of the Angels were born on that day, or previously. However, that's not the only thing I was going to ask.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Mar 18, 2010 11:04 pm @OMF Adam may not have had a choice with the ADO vs LDO environment. Plus his kids all seemed to be functioning just fine in post-2I world. The nursery idea is a new one for me, though. @Legendary The AATF makes people explode into LCL and rips their souls out. It couldn't have torn those people up the way they were shredded. Also notice that Katsuragi somehow has fingers that are stripped clean of flesh. @Merridian But the bodies we see are all a good distance from most of the wreckage, and most of them resemble lumps of meat and cloth at this point. The debris thing crossed my mind too, but then I thought about someone mentioning in the script that Adam's "Chambers of Guf/Gaf/Gauf" were open. Also, I recall someone on screen mentioning that they had flamethrowers on hand to deal with any emerging Angels....

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Mar 18, 2010 11:18 pm GNR's fully-formed, fully-functional AATF caused LCL explosions. Adam was neither, but is CONFIRMED to have released an AATF that turned the sea to blood. If it affected people in a minor way because of their additional complexity, it might be believable.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Mar 19, 2010 12:38 am I agree with the notion that the injuries on the victims of 2I are simply that of what you would expect the general injuries of survivors and victims of hurricanes, or other natural disasters. There did appear to be a lot of debris flying, and infrastructures failing, so it should be no surprise that it looked as though people were going through blenders. As for the Angels, I believe that it was during Adam's short-lived attempt at scrubbing away Lilith-based life off of her planet that Adam was able to lay the fourteen eggs that we later became familiar with as Angels. During the fifteen year period after 2I, the Angels went through the birthing process. We see that with Shamshel, it wasn't truly born until 2015, when Nerv had noticed it, so how do we know that the other Angels weren't hatched just prior to their appearances in the show?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Fri Mar 19, 2010 6:39 am Sachi wrote: I agree with the notion that the injuries on the victims of 2I are simply that of what you would expect the general injuries of survivors and victims of hurricanes, or other natural disasters. There did appear to be a lot of debris flying, and infrastructures failing, so it should be no surprise that it looked as though people were going through blenders.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Fri Mar 19, 2010 8:12 am Well, they were in a scientific facility, and I'm sure there was plenty of chemicals or equipment that could explode or cause fires in those conditions, but you do make a very good point about the possibility of some kind of rampage. We also have this photo, implying that Adam was active and walking around.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Fri Mar 19, 2010 9:28 am Fazmotron wrote: Well, they were in a scientific facility, and I'm sure there was plenty of chemicals or equipment that could explode or cause fires in those conditions, but you do make a very good point about the possibility of some kind of rampage. We also have this photo, implying that Adam was active and walking around.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Mar 19, 2010 10:02 am thermal dampening would imply cooling, not heating.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Fri Mar 19, 2010 10:13 am Yeah, I just checked the script: MAN L: Amazing! It's begun to walk!

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Fri Mar 19, 2010 11:22 am Fazmotron wrote: Yeah, I just checked the script: and then: So its not unlikely that she went for a bit of a walk or a rampage before she went ka-blammo. This only implies that the angels received their souls at this point. And I have no idea what you're talking about with the incineration stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Fri Mar 19, 2010 8:49 pm How so?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 20, 2010 1:29 am I doubt there was much of a rampage. Shortly after Adam starts moving, she unleashes some kind of energy discharge, which is evident from the end of the UN footage, and can be seen from the Moon at the beginning of the #12 flashback. This appears to kill most of the KatsExp on the spot (who knows how Dr. K and Misato survived it) and create the weird multi-colored dome over the South Pole. The dome probably has something to do with the local "eco-alterations". Dr. Katsuragi had apparently already been wounded and beaten a hasty retreat with Misato by the time the final portion of the tape rolls -- note the camera lingering on the bloody glove that's flapping around, which seems to have belonged to Dr. K. Judging from the tape, Adam's AATF had a rather tumultuous effect on the environs, effectively whipping everything up into a heated furor (not unlike the ATs we see on multiple occasions). The effect of the heat and the wind on random crap in the base probably created explosions and other unpleasantness. (BobBQ suggested something about gas pipelines to me once; wish I could remember the specifics.) As for whether the Angels were born on that day, or simply received their souls -- that's open-ended enough to be a "dedicated topic" question. I still have no idea what the stuff about thermal dampening is all about. Maybe applying some kind of effect to Adam through whatever cybernetic horrors they've installed on her, but why wait for the Doors of Guf to open? What does one have to do with the other?

[]:             Quote Post by User-iel » Sat Mar 20, 2010 1:49 am Interesting. So, if we put this together, we're being told the the original SEELE scenario was to somehow hijack Adam, and use her carcass to fashion their new godhead. Consider - why would they develop a mobile restraint system, and then attempt to revive her half way, and stop at that point? Classic 'user privilege escalation' attack. Why build, when you can just re-model?

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sat Mar 20, 2010 4:32 am Could Adam have also tried to unearth her white moon? This could also explain the "eco-alterations" as you put it. This raises another question, is the white moon still intact after 2I, or has it collapsed in on itself, or exploded into blood similar to the black moon during 3I?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 20, 2010 9:10 am Fazmotron wrote: This raises another question, is the white moon still intact after 2I, or has it collapsed in on itself, or exploded into blood similar to the black moon during 3I?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Mar 20, 2010 2:32 pm User-iel wrote: Interesting. So, if we put this together, we're being told the the original SEELE scenario was to somehow hijack Adam, and use her carcass to fashion their new godhead. Consider - why would they develop a mobile restraint system, and then attempt to revive her half way, and stop at that point? Classic 'user privilege escalation' attack. Why build, when you can just re-model?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 20, 2010 2:47 pm AuraTwilight wrote: SEELE intended for Second Impact to happen as it did , which is why they got the hell out of there early.
